title: DevOps & CI/CD - 50 Words
definition: 50 essential terms for continuous integration, deployment, and modern software delivery practices.

words.meaning: A set of practices combining software development and IT operations.
words.sentence: Our DevOps team automated the entire deployment pipeline.

words.meaning: Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment - automating code integration and release.
words.sentence: Our CI/CD pipeline runs tests automatically on every commit.

words.meaning: The practice of frequently merging code changes into a shared repository.
words.sentence: Continuous integration catches conflicts early, before they pile up.

words.meaning: Automatically releasing every code change that passes tests to production.
words.sentence: With continuous deployment, approved changes go live within minutes.

words.meaning: An automated sequence of steps to build, test, and deploy code.
words.sentence: The pipeline failed at the testing stage due to a broken unit test.

words.meaning: The process of converting source code into a runnable application.
words.sentence: The nightly build compiles all our code and runs basic checks.

words.meaning: The process of releasing software to a live environment.
words.sentence: The deployment went smoothly with zero downtime.

words.meaning: Reverting to a previous version of software after a failed release.
words.sentence: We performed a rollback after the new version caused errors.

words.meaning: A release strategy using two environments to minimize downtime during updates.
words.sentence: Blue-green deployment let us switch traffic instantly with zero downtime.

words.meaning: Gradually rolling out a change to a small subset of users first.
words.sentence: We tested the new feature with a canary release to 5% of users.

words.meaning: Managing servers and infrastructure using version-controlled configuration files.
words.sentence: Infrastructure as Code lets us recreate our entire environment from scratch.

words.meaning: A system that tracks changes to code over time.
words.sentence: Version control lets us see exactly who changed this line and when.

words.meaning: A widely used distributed version control system.
words.sentence: We use Git to manage our source code history.

words.meaning: A storage location for a project's code and history.
words.sentence: Clone the repository to get a local copy of the project.

words.meaning: A separate line of development within a repository.
words.sentence: I created a new branch to work on this feature safely.

words.meaning: Combining changes from one branch into another.
words.sentence: We merged the feature branch back into main after review.

words.meaning: A request to merge code changes, often reviewed before approval.
words.sentence: Please review my pull request before I merge it.

words.meaning: A situation where changes from different branches can't be automatically combined.
words.sentence: I had to resolve a merge conflict on the config file.

words.meaning: Using software tools to run tests automatically instead of manually.
words.sentence: Automated testing runs on every pull request before merging.

words.meaning: A test that verifies a single small piece of code works correctly.
words.sentence: This unit test checks that the discount calculation is correct.

words.meaning: A test verifying multiple components work correctly together.
words.sentence: The integration test confirmed the API and database work together properly.

words.meaning: Testing to ensure new changes haven't broken existing functionality.
words.sentence: We run regression tests before every release.

words.meaning: A measure of how much code is exercised by automated tests.
words.sentence: Test coverage for this module is around 85%.

words.meaning: A pre-production environment used to test changes before release.
words.sentence: We deploy to the staging environment before pushing to production.

words.meaning: The live environment where end users interact with the application.
words.sentence: This bug only appears in the production environment, not staging.

words.meaning: A toggle that enables or disables a feature without deploying new code.
words.sentence: We use a feature flag to gradually roll out the new dashboard.

words.meaning: The ongoing observation of system performance and health.
words.sentence: Monitoring alerted us to the spike in error rates immediately.

words.meaning: Recording events and data generated by a system for later review.
words.sentence: Logging helped us trace exactly where the request failed.

words.meaning: Automatically notifying teams when a system issue is detected.
words.sentence: Alerting paged the on-call engineer within seconds of the outage.

words.meaning: A schedule assigning engineers responsibility for handling incidents.
words.sentence: I'm on the on-call rotation this week, so I carry the pager.

words.meaning: A review of an incident to understand its causes and prevent recurrence.
words.sentence: We wrote a post-mortem after the outage to document lessons learned.

words.meaning: The practice of managing system settings consistently across environments.
words.sentence: Configuration management ensures all servers have identical settings.

words.meaning: A packaged output of a build process, ready for deployment.
words.sentence: The build produces a deployable artifact stored in our registry.

words.meaning: A storage system for container images used in deployments.
words.sentence: We push our Docker images to a private container registry.

words.meaning: Automating the coordination of multiple systems or services.
words.sentence: Kubernetes handles the orchestration of our microservices.

words.meaning: An architecture style structuring an app as small, independent services.
words.sentence: We split the monolith into microservices for easier scaling.

words.meaning: A single, large application where all components are tightly coupled.
words.sentence: The old monolith was hard to update without affecting everything.

words.meaning: An approach where servers are replaced rather than modified.
words.sentence: With immutable infrastructure, we deploy new servers instead of patching old ones.

words.meaning: When system configurations gradually diverge from their intended state.
words.sentence: Configuration drift caused inconsistent behavior across our servers.

words.meaning: Documentation describing changes included in a software release.
words.sentence: The release notes explain what's new in this version.

words.meaning: An urgent fix applied directly to production to resolve a critical issue.
words.sentence: We pushed a hotfix to resolve the payment bug within an hour.

words.meaning: A documented set of steps for handling routine or emergency operations.
words.sentence: The runbook walks the on-call engineer through resolving this alert.

words.meaning: Deliberately introducing failures to test system resilience.
words.sentence: Chaos engineering helped us discover a weak point before it caused an outage.

words.meaning: A discipline applying software engineering principles to operations and reliability.
words.sentence: Our SRE team defines the reliability targets for each service.

words.meaning: The allowable amount of downtime or errors before action is required.
words.sentence: We've used up half our error budget for this quarter already.

words.meaning: A metric measuring how often code is deployed to production.
words.sentence: Our deployment frequency increased to several times a day after automating releases.

words.meaning: The time between committing code and it being deployed to production.
words.sentence: Our lead time dropped from days to hours after improving the pipeline.

words.meaning: The average time it takes to restore service after an incident.
words.sentence: Our mean time to recovery improved after adding better alerting.

words.meaning: Storing system configuration in version-controlled files rather than manual settings.
words.sentence: Configuration as code lets us track every infrastructure change in Git.

words.meaning: A quick test verifying the most critical functions work after a build.
words.sentence: The smoke test confirmed the app starts and the login page loads.

quiz.question: What is 'continuous integration'?
options: Frequently merging code changes into a shared repository
Manually testing code once a month
quiz.answer: Frequently merging code changes into a shared repository
quiz.explanation: CI involves regularly integrating code to catch issues early.

quiz.question: What is a 'canary release'?
options: Gradually rolling out a change to a small subset of users first
Deploying to all users at once
quiz.answer: Gradually rolling out a change to a small subset of users first
quiz.explanation: Canary releases test changes on a small group before full rollout.

quiz.question: What is a 'feature flag' used for?
options: Toggling a feature on/off without deploying new code
quiz.answer: Toggling a feature on/off without deploying new code
quiz.explanation: Feature flags let teams enable/disable features dynamically.

quiz.question: A ___ is an urgent fix applied directly to production. (emergency patch)
quiz.explanation: A hotfix quickly resolves a critical production issue.

quiz.question: What does 'mean time to recovery' measure?
options: The average time to restore service after an incident
The number of deployments per day
quiz.answer: The average time to restore service after an incident
quiz.explanation: MTTR measures how quickly a team recovers from failures.
